title: MODELINV model.invoke.complete Payload
description: Audit-event payload for a completed model invocation. cycle-102 Sprint 1 (T1.4 + T1.7). Emitted via cycle-098 audit_emit at the end of every cheval invoke() call. primitive_id=MODELINV per SDD section 4.4 (ASSUMPTION-3 RESOLVED Option B). The payload powers vision-019 M1 silent-degradation audit query: any record with operator_visible_warn=false AND len(models_failed)>0 is, by definition, a silent-degradation event.

models_requested.description: Models the call was launched against. Format: provider:model_id (e.g., openai:gpt-5.5-pro). Pattern bounds the surface area of the audit log against accidental inclusion of arbitrary strings.

models_succeeded.description: Subset of models_requested that returned a usable response (after fallback resolution).

models_failed.description: Per-model failure record. Structurally compatible with the model-error.schema.json envelope but inlined here for audit-query ergonomics. The error_class enum is referenced from model-error.schema.json — keep in sync. cycle-104 Sprint 2 (T2.6): added optional 'provider' (per-entry provider) and 'missing_capabilities' (populated when error_class=CAPABILITY_MISS). Records appear in chain-walk order.

provider.description: cycle-104 Sprint 2 (T2.6 / FR-S2.3). Provider key for this entry (e.g. openai, anthropic, google). Optional for backward compat; new emitters MUST populate.

missing_capabilities.description: cycle-104 Sprint 2 (FR-S2.7). Populated when error_class=CAPABILITY_MISS; lists the capabilities the request required that this entry does not declare.

operator_visible_warn.description: True iff a WARN line was surfaced in the operator-visible header (M7 verification). Audit-query for vision-019 M1 silent-degradation count: operator_visible_warn=false AND len(models_failed)>0.

calling_primitive.description: Optional caller-context attribution per SDD section 4.4. Captures which L1-L7 primitive (if any) initiated the call. Null/absent for direct model-invoke CLI or persona-cheval calls.

capability_class.description: If invoked via class-addressing (e.g., top-reasoning); absent for raw model-id calls.

probe_latency_ms.description: Time spent in probe-gate (cycle-102 Sprint 1 T1.4 split per gemini IMP-006). Allows separation of probe-overhead from actual-call latency for M6 verification.

invocation_latency_ms.description: Time spent in the actual provider call (after probe). Excludes probe time. Sum with probe_latency_ms approximates wall-clock.

cost_micro_usd.description: Total cost across all attempts (primary + fallbacks) in micro-USD (1e-6 USD). Used by L2 cost-budget composition per cycle-098 NFR-Cost1.

kill_switch_active.description: True iff LOA_FORCE_LEGACY_MODELS=1 at invocation time (SDD section 11 / gemini IMP-004 HIGH). Audits operator-driven legacy-mode use.

streaming.description: True iff the call used the Sprint 4A streaming transport (cycle-102 AC-4.5e). False if LOA_CHEVAL_DISABLE_STREAMING=1 routed the call through the legacy non-streaming http_post path. Additive field (Sprint 4A) — older audit entries written before this field existed will simply omit it; readers must handle absence as 'unknown' rather than 'false'.

final_model_id.description: cycle-104 Sprint 2 (T2.6 / FR-S2.3). The model that produced the result, in provider:model_id form. May differ from models_requested[0] when within-company chain walked to a fallback entry. Null/absent when models_succeeded is empty (chain exhausted).

transport.description: cycle-104 Sprint 2 (T2.6). Transport of the FINAL successful entry — derived from its adapter_kind. 'http' for streaming/non-streaming HTTP adapters; 'cli' for headless subprocess adapters (claude-headless, codex-headless, gemini-headless). Null/absent when chain exhausted.

config_observed.description: cycle-104 Sprint 2 (T2.6). Audit-the-mode-source per PRD §1.3 axiom 2. Captures the operator-effective headless routing mode AND the precedence layer it came from (env > config > default), so audit consumers can prove which knob produced the chain walk shape.

role.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F. Caller's logical role at invocation time (PRD §5 FR-2). null/absent for legacy callers that did not pass --role.

tier.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F. Resolved tier — advisor or executor. Null/absent when advisor_strategy disabled or legacy path.

tier_source.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F. Provenance of the tier decision: default (from defaults map), per_skill_override (explicit override), kill_switch (LOA_ADVISOR_STRATEGY_DISABLE or disabled-by-absence).

tier_resolution.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F. Resolution mode at load time. 'static:<sha>' pins to .loa.config.yaml commit SHA (SDD §3.6, FR-9). 'dynamic' re-resolves per call. 'disabled' for legacy path.

sprint_kind.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F. Stratification label for benchmark grouping (e.g. 'glue', 'parser', 'cryptographic'). See SDD §8 taxonomy. Null/absent outside of replay context.

writer_version.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F (SDD §21.4, IMP-012). MODELINV envelope writer version. '1.2' for cycle-108+. Read from .claude/data/cycle-108/modelinv-writer-version SoT file. Null/absent for legacy v1.1 emitters.

invocation_chain.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F (SDD §20.5 ATK-A13). Parent skill / slash-command lineage of this cheval invocation (top-most caller first). Used for post-merge audit of skills claiming review-tier but invoked from non-review chains.

replay_marker.description: cycle-108 sprint-1 T1.F (SDD §20.10 ATK-A15). True when this invocation originates inside a benchmark replay context (LOA_REPLAY_CONTEXT=1). Rollup tool excludes replay-marked envelopes from prod queries by default.

pricing_snapshot.description:
cycle-108 sprint-2 T2.J (SDD §20.9 ATK-A20). Pricing captured at invocation time from .claude/defaults/model-config.yaml::providers.

.models..pricing. Units: micro-USD per million tokens. Rollup tool reads pricing FROM envelopes (not current model-config), so historical pricing changes do not retroactively rewrite cost reports.

capability_evaluation.description: cycle-109 Sprint 1 T1.4 (SDD §3.3.1). Snapshot of the capability-aware substrate pre-flight gate decision for THIS invocation. Optional/additive — legacy emitters and unmigrated callers omit the field; v1.3 emitters populate when the pre-flight gate ran (whether it preempted, dispatched, or routed through chunking). preflight_decision enum: 'dispatch' = passthrough to chain walk; 'preempt' = exit 7 ContextTooLarge emitted before adapter dispatch; 'chunk' = routed through Sprint 4 chunking primitive. effective_input_ceiling MAY be null when the head chain entry's model-config is still v2-shape (gate disabled but evaluation ran). recommended_for echoes the v3 capability surface verbatim (allow-all default `[review, audit, implementation, dissent, arbiter]` per SKP-004 v5 closure).

verdict_quality.description: cycle-109 Sprint 2 T2.3 (SDD §3.3.1 v1.3 additive + §3.2 verdict-quality.schema.json). First-class envelope describing voices succeeded/dropped, chain health, and confidence for THIS substrate invocation. Inlined structurally rather than $ref'd so the MODELINV schema is self-contained — verdict-quality.schema.json remains the authority for envelope-alone validation in producer paths. Optional/additive: pre-T2.3 callers and consumers that don't yet emit the envelope simply omit the field. NFR-Rel-1: when present, the inline structure rejects status values outside {APPROVED, DEGRADED, FAILED} — `clean` is definitionally impossible.

chunked_review.description: cycle-109 Sprint 4 T4.7 (SDD §3.3.1 v1.3 additive + §5.4). Snapshot of the chunked-review dispatch state when the pre-flight gate routed input through the chunking package (loa_cheval.chunking). Absent when chunking was not invoked. Operator-facing PR-comment header (T4.8) renders this distinctly from the overall verdict so chunk-count + per-chunk degradation is visible.

streaming_recovery.description: cycle-109 Sprint 4 T4.7 (SDD §5.4.4 / IMP-014). Streaming-with-recovery telemetry: triggered = true when one of the three thresholds (first-token-deadline / empty-content-window / CoT-budget) aborted the stream. tokens_before_abort + reason populated only when triggered = true.

auth_type_resolved.description: Cycle-110 sprint-2b1 T2.8 (FR-3.4): auth_type bucket the resolver SELECTED for this invocation. Bedrock entries use aws_iam; HTTP-API providers use http_api; subscription-CLI providers use headless. **SELECTED, not FULFILLED**: this is the FIRST chain entry's auth_type after dispatch_preference filtering; on chain-walk failover the actually-succeeding entry may have a different auth_type (visible via final_model_id + model-config lookup). BB #907 iter-1 F-007 explicit clarification. v1.3 readers tolerate this additive field; absence means pre-cycle-110 entry — readers MUST default to http_api per cycle-110 SDD §3.4 backward-compat.

auth_type_selection_reason.description: Cycle-110 sprint-2b1 T2.8 (FR-3.4): how the auth_type was chosen. explicit-dispatch_preference = operator set dispatch_preference to a concrete value; auto-band-comparison = auto-mode used warm-window stats; auto-cold-start-recommended_for = capability_evaluation.recommended_for hint; auto-cold-start-default-headless = no warm data and no hint, defaulted to headless when available.

auto_selection_inputs.description: Cycle-110 sprint-2b1 T2.8 (FR-3.4 / SDD §5.5 [Q7] resolution). C11 carry-in: canonical example shape pinned by tests/test_dispatch_filter.py::TestAutoResolutionEnvelopeShape. Present ONLY when auth_type_selection_reason == 'auto-band-comparison'. Aggregate-only counters; no operator content. NFR-Sec-4 redaction pass still runs as defense-in-depth.

auto_evaluation_timestamp.description: Cycle-110 sprint-2b1 T2.8 (C10 carry-in): epoch-seconds when the auto-mode algorithm ran. FROZEN at evaluation time so retrospective config changes don't shift the audit-trail interpretation. Present only when auth_type_selection_reason starts with 'auto-'.

semaphore_exhausted.description: Cycle-110 sprint-2b1 T2.8 (C12 carry-in): true when the headless-concurrency semaphore (sprint-2b2 T2.11) rejected the call after exhausting bounded retries; distinct from chain_exhausted. Future-compat — sprint-2b2 wires the actual emitter; sprint-2b1 only declares the schema slot so the readers can be coded against it now.
